---
title: "Chapter 11 - Vishvarupa-Darshana Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">विश्वरूपदर्शनयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-10.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-12.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-11-1" data-reference="11.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-1">11.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Out of grace toward me, you have spoken this supreme secret, known as the teaching of the Self; by those words, this delusion of mine has now been dispelled.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम् ।<br>
यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-2" data-reference="11.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-2">11.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I have heard in detail from you of the arising and dissolution of beings, and also of your unfailing majesty, O lotus-eyed one.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया ।<br>
त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-3" data-reference="11.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-3">11.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus, as You describe Yourself, O supreme Lord, I wish to see Your divine form, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purushottama</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर ।<br>
द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-4" data-reference="11.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-4">11.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If you think that I can see it, O Lord, then show me your imperishable Self, O Yogeshvara.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो ।<br>
योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयात्मानमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-5" data-reference="11.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-5">11.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Behold, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, my forms in hundreds and thousands, of many kinds, divine, and of many colors and shapes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।<br>
नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-6" data-reference="11.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-6">11.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">See the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the Aśvins, and the Maruts as well.<br>
O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, see many wonders never seen before.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्विनौ मरुतस्तथा ।<br>
बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याश्चर्याणि भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-7" data-reference="11.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-7">11.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Here, in one place, see today the whole universe, with all that moves and does not move, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." aria-label="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." title="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." data-glossary-id="gudakesha">Guḍākeśa</span>; and whatever else you wish to see in My body.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम् ।<br>
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद् द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-8" data-reference="11.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-8">11.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But you cannot see Me with these, your own eyes. I give you divine vision; behold My divine <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा ।<br>
दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-9" data-reference="11.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-9">11.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having spoken thus, O king, Hari, the great Lord of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, then showed to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span> His supreme divine form.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः ।<br>
दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-10" data-reference="11.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-10">11.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">with many faces and eyes, and many wondrous forms; adorned with many divine ornaments, and bearing many uplifted divine weapons.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम् ।<br>
अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-11" data-reference="11.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-11">11.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Wearing divine garlands and garments, anointed with divine fragrances,<br>
the wondrous god, infinite, with faces on every side.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम् ।<br>
सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-12" data-reference="11.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-12">11.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If a thousand suns were to rise together in the sky, that might be like the splendor of that great soul.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता ।<br>
यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-13" data-reference="11.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-13">11.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pāṇḍava</span> then saw the entire universe, all in one place, yet divided in many ways, in the body of the god of gods.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा ।<br>
अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-14" data-reference="11.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-14">11.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Then <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>, filled with wonder, his hair standing on end, bowed his head to the Deva with joined palms and spoke.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः ।<br>
प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-15" data-reference="11.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-15">11.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see all the gods in your body, O God, and all the diverse host of beings; Brahma seated on the lotus, the sages, and all the divine serpents.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे<br>
सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान् ।<br>
ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थ-<br>
मृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-16" data-reference="11.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-16">11.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see You with many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes,<br>
with forms unending on every side.<br>
I see no end, no middle, and no beginning of You,<br>
O Lord of the universe, O universal form.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं<br>
पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम् ।<br>
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं<br>
पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-17" data-reference="11.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-17">11.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see you with a crown, a mace, and a discus,<br>
a mass of radiance, shining on all sides,<br>
hard to gaze upon from every direction,<br>
immeasurable in splendor, like blazing fire and the sun.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च<br>
तेजोराशिं सर्वतो दीप्तिमन्तम् ।<br>
पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्<br>
दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-18" data-reference="11.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-18">11.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are the imperishable supreme reality to be known;<br>
You are the ultimate refuge of this universe.<br>
You are the imperishable guardian of eternal <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>;<br>
I regard You as the eternal <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purusha</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं<br>
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।<br>
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता<br>
सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-19" data-reference="11.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-19">11.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see you as without beginning, middle, or end, of infinite power, with endless arms and with eyes like the moon and the sun, with a face of blazing fire, scorching this entire universe with your own radiance.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्य-<br>
मनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम् ।<br>
पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रं<br>
स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-20" data-reference="11.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-20">11.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Indeed, this whole space between heaven and earth and all the directions is pervaded by you alone. Seeing this wondrous, terrible form of yours, the three worlds are shaken, O Mahātman.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि<br>
व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः ।<br>
दृष्ट्वाद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं<br>
लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-21" data-reference="11.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-21">11.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Indeed, these hosts of the gods enter you;<br>
some, भयभीत, with folded hands praise you.<br>
The hosts of great sages and Siddhas, saying “svasti,”<br>
extol you with abundant hymns.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घा विशन्ति<br>
केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति ।<br>
स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः<br>
स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-22" data-reference="11.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-22">11.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The Rudras, the Adityas, the Vasus, and the Sadhyas,<br>
the Viśve, the Aśvins, the Maruts, and the Uṣmapās;<br>
the Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas—<br>
all of them gaze at you in wonder.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या<br>
विश्वेऽश्विनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च ।<br>
गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा<br>
वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-23" data-reference="11.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-23">11.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Your great form, O mighty-armed one, has many faces and eyes,<br>
many arms, thighs, and feet;<br>
many bellies, and terrible with many teeth.<br>
Seeing this, the worlds are shaken, and so am I.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं<br>
महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम् ।<br>
बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं<br>
दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-24" data-reference="11.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-24">11.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Touching the sky, blazing, of many colors, with open mouths and blazing, vast eyes,<br>
seeing you, my inner self is shaken; I find neither steadiness nor peace, O Vishnu.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं<br>
व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम् ।<br>
दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा<br>
धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-25" data-reference="11.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-25">11.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Seeing your faces, terrible with fangs and resembling the fire of time, I know not the directions and find no peace. Be gracious, O Lord of the gods, abode of the universe.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि<br>
दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि ।<br>
दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म<br>
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-26" data-reference="11.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-26">11.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And these, the sons of Dhritarashtra, all together with the hosts of kings of the earth: Bhishma, Drona, and also that son of Suta, together with our chief warriors.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः<br>
सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः ।<br>
भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ<br>
सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-27" data-reference="11.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-27">11.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see Your mouths, terrible with fangs, swallowing them in haste;<br>
some are seen with their heads crushed, caught between the spaces of the teeth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति<br>
दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि ।<br>
केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु<br>
सन्दृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-28" data-reference="11.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-28">11.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As many currents of rivers rush toward the sea,<br>
so these heroes of the human world enter your blazing mouths.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः<br>
समुद्रमेवाभिमुखा द्रवन्ति ।<br>
तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा<br>
विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-29" data-reference="11.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-29">11.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As moths with great speed enter a blazing flame to their destruction, so too the worlds, with great speed, enter your mouths to destruction.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा<br>
विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः ।<br>
तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोका-<br>
स्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-30" data-reference="11.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-30">11.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are licking up all the worlds on every side with your flaming mouths,<br>
filling the entire universe with your radiance.<br>
Your fierce rays are scorching the whole world, O Vishnu.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ताल्-<br>
लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः ।<br>
तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं<br>
भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-31" data-reference="11.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-31">11.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Tell me who you are, O fierce-formed one.<br>
Homage to you, best of the devas; be gracious.<br>
I wish to know you, the primal one;<br>
for I do not understand your activity.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो<br>
नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद ।<br>
विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं<br>
न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-32" data-reference="11.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-32">11.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am Time, grown to maturity, the destroyer of the worlds, engaged here in gathering the worlds together. Even without you, all the warriors stationed in the opposing armies will not survive.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो<br>
लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः ।<br>
ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे<br>
येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-33" data-reference="11.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-33">11.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore arise and win renown;<br>
conquer your enemies and enjoy a prosperous kingdom.<br>
They have already been slain by me;<br>
be only the instrument, O Savyasacin.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व<br>
जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम् ।<br>
मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव<br>
निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-34" data-reference="11.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-34">11.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Drona and Bhishma and Jayadratha,<br>
Karna, and other valiant warriors too—<br>
slay those who have already been slain by me;<br>
do not be troubled. Fight, and you will conquer your foes in battle.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च<br>
कर्णं तथान्यानपि योधवीरान् ।<br>
मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा<br>
युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-35" data-reference="11.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-35">11.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Hearing these words of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span>, Kiriti, with folded hands and trembling, bowed down to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span> again and again. In fear and with a choked voice, he prostrated himself.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य<br>
कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी ।<br>
नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं<br>
सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-36" data-reference="11.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-36">11.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Rightly, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span>, the world rejoices and delights in your glory; the fearful rakshasas flee in all directions, and all the hosts of siddhas bow down.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या<br>
जगत्प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च ।<br>
रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति<br>
सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-37" data-reference="11.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-37">11.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And why should they not bow to you, O Mahatman, greater than even <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, the primal creator?<br>
O infinite one, Lord of the gods, abode of the world, you are the imperishable, the supreme reality beyond being and non-being.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्<br>
गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे ।<br>
अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास<br>
त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-38" data-reference="11.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-38">11.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are the original Deva, the ancient <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purusha</span>,<br>
the supreme refuge of this universe.<br>
You are the knower and the knowable, and the supreme abode;<br>
the universe is pervaded by you, whose forms are endless.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराण-<br>
स्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।<br>
वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम<br>
त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-39" data-reference="11.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-39">11.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are Vayu, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the Moon, Prajapati, and the great-grandfather.<br>
Obeisance to you a thousand times; again and again, obeisance to you.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः<br>
प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च ।<br>
नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः<br>
पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-40" data-reference="11.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-40">11.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Obeisance to you from the front and from behind;<br>
obeisance to you on all sides, O all.<br>
You are of infinite power and immeasurable prowess;<br>
you pervade all, and so you are all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते<br>
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व ।<br>
अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं<br>
सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-41" data-reference="11.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-41">11.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thinking of You as a friend, I addressed You rashly, saying, “O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, O Yadava, O friend,”<br>
not knowing this greatness of Yours,<br>
whether through carelessness or through affection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं<br>
हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति ।<br>
अजानता महिमानं तवेदं<br>
मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-42" data-reference="11.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-42">11.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever I may have treated you with disrespect, in jest or through familiarity, in recreation, in bed, at rest, or at meals, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." aria-label="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." title="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." data-glossary-id="acyuta">Acyuta</span>, whether alone or in the presence of others—I beg forgiveness from you, the immeasurable One.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि<br>
विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु ।<br>
एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं<br>
तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-43" data-reference="11.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-43">11.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are the father of the moving and unmoving world,<br>
its object of reverence and the greatest guru.<br>
There is none equal to you; how then could there be anyone greater than you,<br>
unrivaled in power in the three worlds.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य<br>
त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान् ।<br>
न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो<br>
लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-44" data-reference="11.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-44">11.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore, bowing down and laying my body low, I beg You, O Lord worthy of praise, to be gracious to me. Like a father to his son, like a friend to his friend, like one dear to one dear, O God, You should endure this.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं<br>
प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम् ।<br>
पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः<br>
प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-45" data-reference="11.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-45">11.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Seeing what I had never seen before, I am filled with delight, yet my mind is also shaken with fear.<br>
Show me again that very form, O Deva; be gracious, O Lord of the gods, abode of the universe.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा<br>
भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे ।<br>
तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपं<br>
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-46" data-reference="11.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-46">11.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I wish to see you, crowned, holding the mace and discus,<br>
just as before, in that same four-armed form.<br>
O thousand-armed one, O universal form, appear thus.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तं<br>
इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव ।<br>
तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन<br>
सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-47" data-reference="11.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-47">11.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Out of favor toward you, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, I have shown this supreme form by my own <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>: the radiant, infinite, primal universal form, which none before you has ever seen.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं<br>
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात् ।<br>
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं<br>
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-48" data-reference="11.48">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-48">11.48</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Neither by study of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> and sacrifices, nor by gifts, nor by rites, nor by severe austerities can I be seen in this human world in this form, O foremost of the Kurus, by anyone other than you.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानै-<br>
र्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः ।<br>
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके<br>
द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-49" data-reference="11.49">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-49">11.49</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Do not be troubled, and do not be bewildered at seeing this terrible form of Mine.<br>
With fear dispelled and your mind made glad, look again upon this very form of Mine.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो<br>
दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम् ।<br>
व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं<br>
तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-50" data-reference="11.50">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-50">11.50</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus, having spoken thus to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." aria-label="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." title="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." data-glossary-id="vasudeva">Vasudeva</span> once more revealed his own form. Then the great-souled one, taking on a gentle form again, reassured this terrified one.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा<br>
स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः ।<br>
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं<br>
भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-51" data-reference="11.51">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-51">11.51</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Seeing this gentle human form of Yours, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." title="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="janardana">Janārdana</span>,<br>
I have now come back to my senses and returned to my nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन ।<br>
इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-52" data-reference="11.52">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-52">11.52</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This form of Mine, which you have seen, is exceedingly difficult to behold; even the gods are always eager to see this form.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम ।<br>
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-53" data-reference="11.53">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-53">11.53</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Neither by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, nor by austerity, nor by charity, nor by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as you have seen Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया ।<br>
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-54" data-reference="11.54">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-54">11.54</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But by exclusive devotion, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, I can be known and seen in this form and entered into in truth, O scorcher of foes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन ।<br>
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-11-55" data-reference="11.55">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-11-55">11.55</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whoever does work for Me, regards Me as the highest, is devoted to Me, and is free from attachment, free from enmity toward all beings, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pāṇḍava</span>, comes to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः ।<br>
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
